collections/Counter/subtract.name: subtract
collections/Counter/subtract.description: Elements are subtracted from an iterable or from another mapping (or counter). Like dict.update() but subtracts counts instead of replacing them. Both inputs and outputs may be zero or negative. >>> c = Counter(a=4, b=2, c=0, d=-2) >>> d = Counter(a=1, b=2, c=3, d=4) >>> c.subtract(d) >>> c Counter({'a': 3, 'b': 0, 'c': -3, 'd': -6}) Added in version 3.2.
